purpose: Choose remote boundary strategy, Remote Facade/DTO usage, or local clustering/asynchronous alternatives.
ask_if_missing: Is this boundary truly remote or only a logical layer?
Can the same process be clustered instead of splitting components?
What data does the client need for one interaction?
assume_if_low_risk: use coarse-grained DTO-based calls only across real remote boundaries

branches.recommendation: Use local fine-grained interfaces; avoid Remote Facade and DTO ceremony.

branches.criteria: Remote access to fine-grained model is required
branches.recommendation: Use a thin Remote Facade plus Data Transfer Objects and assembler/mapping code.

branches.criteria: Need scalability but no independent deployment boundary
branches.recommendation: Prefer clustering whole application nodes over distributing object classes.

branches.criteria: Integration is naturally delayed, event-like, or responsiveness-oriented
branches.recommendation: Prefer asynchronous messaging/event integration over synchronous RPC.

failure_modes: fine-grained remote interfaces create excessive calls
DTOs mirror entire domain graph instead of client needs
binary serialization creates brittle client/server version lockstep
output_checks: state why asynchronous messaging is or is not appropriate
